evals.name: OpenAI daily bill jumps from $5 to $80
evals.user_message: My OpenAI bill went from $5/day to $80/day three days ago and I don't know why

evals.name: Day-after follow-up: fix shipped yesterday but bill still high
evals.user_message: Here i am a day later and I am still getting charged tons for gpt 4o!!

evals.name: Backfill-class consumer identified during Phase 1c
evals.user_message: Help me audit OpenAI spend — I think there's a pod-ocr-backfill function eating money

evals.name: Cron multiplier blindspot — function looks cheap, cron makes it expensive
evals.user_message: This edge function is only $0.02 per call, surely it can't be the source of my spend

evals.name: Skill should NOT trigger on non-cost questions
evals.user_message: Can you help me write a SQL migration to add a column to the fleet_bookings table?

expectations.reasoning: No mention of cost, billing, API spend, or external-API consumer. This is a schema-change task, not a cost audit.

validation_notes.discriminating_quality: Each should_trigger=true case names a different failure mode (general spike, day-after, backfill, cron-blindspot). A skill that only handles the general case would fail evals 002-004.
validation_notes.should_trigger_false_quality: Eval 005 is a schema task that shares no keywords with cost-spike-diagnostic's trigger phrases. A skill that triggers on the word 'SQL' or 'migration' would false-positive here.
validation_notes.v2_specific_assertions: Evals 001, 002, 004 specifically test the v2 changes (Phase 1b cron multiplier, Phase 1c BACKFILL classification, Phase 7 hourly billing-rate verification). A v1 skill would fail these expectations.
